entry.title: Re: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content:
Hello @ekekakos ,

You can use CTE's if your abap version is above 7.51. Then you will be able to use the distinct operator on the dates and a cross join with the airlines.
This is also doable without cte's. By fetching the data and looping over each date and every airline. I have included both methods.

CLASS zcl_flights DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_alv,
              carrid TYPE /dmo/carrier_id,
              fldate TYPE /dmo/flight_date,
              name   TYPE /dmo/first_name,
            END OF ty_alv.

    TYPES tt_alv TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_alv WITH EMPTY KEY.

    METHODS constructor.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    DATA gt_flights TYPE tt_alv.

    METHODS get_flights_with_cte
      RETURNING VALUE(rt_flights) TYPE tt_alv.

    METHODS get_flights_with_abap
      RETURNING VALUE(rt_flights) TYPE tt_alv.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_flights IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_flights_with_cte.

    WITH

    +lt_distinct_flight_dates AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT
            fldate
        FROM
            zek_sbook
    ),

    +lt_date_carrier_cross_product AS (
        SELECT
            a~carrid,
            d~fldate
        FROM
            zek_scarr AS a
        CROSS JOIN
            +lt_distinct_flight_dates AS d
    ),

    +lt_result AS (
        SELECT
            f~carrid,
            f~fldate,
            a~name
        FROM
            +lt_date_carrier_cross_product AS f
        LEFT OUTER JOIN
            zek_sbook AS a
        ON
            f~carrid = a~carrid AND
            f~fldate = a~fldate
    )

    SELECT * FROM +lt_result ORDER BY fldate, carrid INTO TABLE @rt_flights.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_flights_with_abap.

    SELECT * FROM zek_sbook INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_bookings).

    SELECT carrid FROM zek_scarr ORDER BY carrid INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_carriers).

    SELECT fldate FROM zek_sbook ORDER BY fldate INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_dates).

    DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_dates COMPARING fldate.

    LOOP AT lt_dates INTO DATA(lv_date).
      LOOP AT lt_carriers INTO DATA(lv_carrier).

        APPEND VALUE ty_alv(
            carrid = lv_carrier
            fldate = lv_date
            name   = COND #( 
                        WHEN line_exists( lt_bookings[ carrid = lv_carrier fldate = lv_date ] )
                        THEN lt_bookings[ carrid = lv_carrier fldate = lv_date ]-name
                     )
        ) TO rt_flights.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 Below is the local test class so you can inject test data and check yourself.

*"* use this source file for your ABAP unit test classes
CLASS ltc_flights DEFINITION FOR TESTING
 RISK LEVEL HARMLESS
 DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    DATA mo_cut TYPE REF TO zcl_flights.

    CLASS-DATA go_sql_enviroment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment.

    CLASS-METHODS class_setup.
    CLASS-METHODS class_teardown.
    METHODS setup.
    METHODS teardown.

    METHODS test_flights_with_cte  FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.
    METHODS test_flights_with_abap FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_flights DEFINITION LOCAL FRIENDS ltc_flights.

CLASS ltc_flights IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD class_setup.
    go_sql_enviroment =
      cl_osql_test_environment=>create(
      i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'ZEK_SBOOK' ) ( 'ZEK_SCARR' ) )
    ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD class_teardown.
    go_sql_enviroment->destroy( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD setup.
    mo_cut = NEW #(  ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD teardown.
    go_sql_enviroment->clear_doubles(  ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_flights_with_cte.
    DATA lt_expected TYPE zcl_flights=>tt_alv.
    DATA lt_bookings TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zek_sbook.
    DATA lt_carriers TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zek_scarr.

    lt_bookings = VALUE #(
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'ELIAS' seats = 5 )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'MARIA' seats = 2 )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = 'JOHN'  seats = 3 )
    ).

    lt_carriers = VALUE #(
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' air_name = 'AIRLINE_1' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AB' air_name = 'AIRLINE_2' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AF' air_name = 'AIRLINE_3' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'BE' air_name = 'AIRLINE_4' )
    ).

    go_sql_enviroment->insert_test_data(
      EXPORTING
        i_data = lt_bookings
    ).

    go_sql_enviroment->insert_test_data(
      EXPORTING
        i_data = lt_carriers
    ).

    lt_expected = VALUE #(
        ( carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'ELIAS' )
        ( carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'MARIA' )
        ( carrid = 'AF' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'BE' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = 'JOHN' )
        ( carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'AF' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'BE' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
    ).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act = mo_cut->get_flights_with_cte( )
        exp = lt_expected
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_flights_with_abap.
    DATA lt_expected TYPE zcl_flights=>tt_alv.
    DATA lt_bookings TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zek_sbook.
    DATA lt_carriers TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zek_scarr.

    lt_bookings = VALUE #(
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'ELIAS' seats = 5 )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'MARIA' seats = 2 )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = 'JOHN'  seats = 3 )
    ).

    lt_carriers = VALUE #(
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AA' air_name = 'AIRLINE_1' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AB' air_name = 'AIRLINE_2' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'AF' air_name = 'AIRLINE_3' )
        ( client = 100 carrid = 'BE' air_name = 'AIRLINE_4' )
    ).

    go_sql_enviroment->insert_test_data(
      EXPORTING
        i_data = lt_bookings
    ).

    go_sql_enviroment->insert_test_data(
      EXPORTING
        i_data = lt_carriers
    ).

    lt_expected = VALUE #(
        ( carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'ELIAS' )
        ( carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = 'MARIA' )
        ( carrid = 'AF' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'BE' fldate = CONV #( '20240530' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'AA' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = 'JOHN' )
        ( carrid = 'AB' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'AF' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
        ( carrid = 'BE' fldate = CONV #( '20240531' ) name = '' )
    ).


    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act = mo_cut->get_flights_with_abap( )
        exp = lt_expected
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.


ENDCLASS.

entry.title: What to do if monospace characters in SAP GUI become 'ugly'
entry.content:
As an SAP support engineer, I frequently access many different systems a day.

And I suppose that it is after accessing a system that SAP GUI settings change in such a way that an annoying font starts appearing as the default one for monospace characters.

In the first time the issue happened to me, it took many months for the font to return to normal.

In the second time, I didn't count on patience but instead tried to figure out what was going on.
It was particularly frustrating because not even font settings for SAP GUI would solve the case!
Fortunately, I found the answer and I'd like to publicly share it in this blog post.

Root cause is that the character set for SAP GUI changed.

You can check this discussion to understand the problem of how Eastern characters and Western characters do not have matching "shapes".
While Western characters can be as thin as "I", Eastern characters tend to have equal widths.
And so, it is particularly challenging to find a monospace font that handles both Eastern and Western characters very well.

As you can see, the resulting font disposition for SAP GUI is not aesthetically pleasing.

In other words, it seems ugly! How to get rid of that?

1) Go to program regedit:

2) Go to path HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\SAP\SAPGUI
Front\SAP Frontend Server\FontSettings\ and set CurrentCharSet to 0:

In my case, it was set as 129 (Korean) before. Number 0 stands for 'West European', which is probably the same one you're looking for.

Otherwise, you can check page 33 of this documentation for other character sets.
That page also explains the REGEDIT setting above.

entry.title: Re: Adapt UI activate and publish button not enabled - S4...
entry.content:
Dear Raavi,

 

The buttons are only available when you make changes to the UI. 

You can see in the screenshot that the status of the UI is "Original App", this status means that no changes were made.

When you make changes to the UI, the status changes to "Draft" and the button "Activate new version" will be available.

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: how to check business catalog to be assigned For cust...
entry.content:
Dear Ruchi,

 

You need to assign one or more Business Catalogs to the Custom Catalog Extension related to the Application.

You can choose any Business Catalog that you want. It can either be a catalog related to Extensibility/Custom Analytical Queries or a catalog related to the datasources that you're using.

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Unlock the full potential of SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @MaurizioSantoro,

Feeling and experience when using cloud applications like SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere yes, as you explained - it's a lot of customization but very little add-hoc development. I agree with you that the statement should not be true! The cloud apps have lots of ad-hoc development capabilities with code or without code (visual). On the other hand, we need to think about why experience is not well taken especially for admins and developers. Because cloud apps like that if everything works it's fine, but if something is wrong God may help you. It means that monitoring and error mechanism and troubleshooting support are missing some valuable user experience. You cannot debug the cloud application. That's fine, but at least we deserve better error and log documentation for troubleshooting, etc. That's one thing that is still -I guess- valid for about the cloud apps, though there are a lot of improvements release by release.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: Customized fields at Case management of FSCM
entry.content:
Hi Jigang,

I followed below steps to trigger the BADI - UKM_DCD_MAINTAIN.

Issue: In my case, we recently migrated to S4. When user tries to use search criteria on field 'Next Date' nothing was showing up.

Solution:

First we need to update the values in UKM_DCD_ATTR table using BADI - UKM_DCD_MAINTAIN~MAINTAIN.

1) Go to SE38 and keep the external break point for user 'SAP_WFRT'.
 2) Go to class 'CL_UKM_DCD_WF_EVENT', click on display, put the break point in method - BI_EVENT_HANDLER_STATIC~ON_EVENT.
3) Go to class method ->cl_ukm_xi_facade=>if_ukm_dcd_push-push_dcd  ( line no 56 ).
4) BADI 'UKM_DCD_MAINTAIN' gets triggered after sales order saved and DB updates.

  Update the structure cs_add_attributes-CMNGV1 value then table UKM_DCD_ATTR gets updated.

Thank You,

Srini.

entry.title: Re: Payment Term configuration not obtaining the desired...
entry.content:
Hi,

Your term from Document date is "Net 30 days on the 10th of the month".

In tx. OBB8, you may try to configure a term with settings in the "Payment Terms" section as

Percentage: 0,000

No. of Days: 30

Fixed Day: 10

Additional months: 0 

Note that under "Baseline date for calculation", "Fixed Date" and  "Additional Months" should be left as '0' and the "Default for baseline date" should be the Document Date.

In this way, your actual calculated due date would be the 10th and could vary between 30 to 59 days from Document date.

Kind regards,

Georgios

entry.title: Re: Mark Prices of Actual Costing Ledger Run error
entry.content:
Hi Omega,

The “Mark Prices” step in the “Edit Actual Costing Runs” App is only available in the future. The specified period and year must be in the future, relative to today's date, not based on any other date parameters that might be used in the system. Since in your screens you’re using period 3 the system is issuing the error message “Marking date is in the past” (C+406) to let you know that is not possible to run the step using March 2024. In order to execute the step you would have to use July 2024 (since we’re in June).

That said, even when the system can use actual cost and perform this step every period, is common to see that the prices are Marked just once every year to keep stability in the Standard prices.

For more information I recommend you to follow (at least) the Test Script of the scope item Actual Costing (33Q) Please note that I'm sharing the link to the US, if you're using a different country please update it once you open the link.

I hope this helps.

 

Ernesto Cepeda

entry.title: Architecting Success: Navigating successful SAP Enterprise Architect Certification(SAP EA) Journey
entry.content:
Enterprise architects play a vital role in guiding customer leaders through the digital transformation journey, offering expertise in both business and IT transformation. This requires proficiency in understanding new business models and the ability to discuss technology stacks, including Cloud, landscape strategy, Artificial intelligence, Internet of Things, and more.

In support of companies looking to future-proof their technology environments, SAP introduced the SAP Certified Professional - SAP Enterprise Architect certification last year. I successfully passed the exam with curated preparation. I have since received inquiries from SAP consultants worldwide seeking guidance on how to prepare for the exam. I thought of writing this article which can be beneficial for fellow consultants and architects who want to embark on this journey.

Undoubtedly, this certification is exceptionally challenging due to two main factors:

  • The extensive range of concepts covered in the exam, encompasses SAP Enterprise architecture methodology, SAP EA tools, Business architecture, Data architecture, Technology architecture, Application architecture, and more.
  • The passing score required for certification is set at 80%.

About the Exam

The examination comprises 40 questions divided into two sections: the initial section consists of 22 multiple-choice questions, while the subsequent section consists of 18 questions centered around a case study.

For more information about the exam, including details and a guide, please refer to the link below. It provides insights into skill areas along with weightage and the Wanderlust case study.

https://learning.sap.com/enterprise-architect

Skill Areas measured and its weightage:

SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework and Toolset 20-30 %

Architecture Vision and Roadmap 20-30 %

Business Architecture 20-30 %

Data, Application, and Technology Architecture 20-30%

Exam preparation recommendations and guides

While not mandatory, it is advisable to pursue TOGAF certification before undertaking the SAP Enterprise Architect certification since the SAP EA framework is constructed upon TOGAF. A solid grasp of the architecture framework and Architecture Development Method is essential.

There is a recommended training course, (SAP Intelligent enterprise-Architecture foundation). I could not find time to go through this training but I successfully passed the exam through the following preparation.

SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework(SAP EAF) and Toolset

  • A clear understanding of SAP EA Methodology which is an EA developmental process based on TOGAF ADM is critical.
  • Ensure you understand the SAP recommended artifacts (Both Mandatory and optional artifacts) at each phase from Architecture vision to architecture change governance
  • Go through this wonderful blog from Rene de Daniel sharing the links on different artifacts and their usage in SAP EAF
  • Check the blog on SAP EAF to get a deeper understanding of SAP EA Framework
  • SAP offers various tools to access Reference Business Content (SAP RBA) and Reference Solution Content (SAP RSA). Familiarizing yourself with the functionalities of these tools, such as SAP Signavio, SAP Business Accelerator Hub, SAP EAD, etc., can prove beneficial. As a SAP Enterprise Architect, it is expected that you efficiently explore these tools for the most up-to-date business and solution contents

Architecture Vision and Roadmap

  • Ensure clarity regarding the steps involved in Architectural Vision and Roadmap, including essential architectural work products.
  • It is vital to share a common understanding of business strategy elements such as vision, goals, etc., and to comprehend the sustainable business model canvas. The exam aims to evaluate your approach to solving a business transformation use case and deriving the corresponding business model. Pay attention to the sequence of the Business Model Canvas (BMC).
  • Examine architectural principles, their underlying rationale, and Transition Architectures as part of your preparation.

Business architecture

  • Crucial: Familiarize yourself with both the capability-centric and process-centric approaches for developing the target architecture. Understand the methodology for transitioning from Baseline Business Architecture to Target Business Architecture.
  • Essential concepts like the Business Footprint Diagram, Gap Analysis, and Stakeholder Review must be thoroughly understood and mastered before undertaking the exam.

Data, Application, and Technology Architecture

SAP enterprise architects are anticipated to possess comprehensive knowledge of end-to-end SAP technologies, covering Data, Application, and Technology architecture along with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP PaaS). Gain a deep understanding of crucial topics including SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Extension Methodology, SAP Clean Core Principles, Side-by-Side Extensions, Key User, and On-Stack Extensions, among others.

Integration architecture

The below guide offers comprehensive information on SAP ISA-M methodology, covering process integration, data integration, analytics, and Thing integration. Make sure to review the entire guide for a clearer understanding of SAP integration methodology and approach.

Integration architecture CIO guide

Extension architecture

Most SAP customers employ extension methodologies to expand SAP applications, encompassing key user extensibility, developer extensibility, side-by-side extensions, and more. It is essential to understand these diverse topics and how customers can leverage them for their specific scenarios. Familiarity with various options like SAP ABAP environment, SAP Build, SAP Business Application Studio, SAP Event Mesh, Advanced Event Mesh, etc., would be beneficial. Refer to the provided guide for a comprehensive understanding of the end-to-end extension architecture.

Extensions in S/4HANA implementation-Clean core and BTP

Technology architecture

In your role as an enterprise architect, it is your responsibility to advise customers on IT architecture, determining factors such as the required number of instances and whether they should be global or regional based on the customer's business processes. Refer to the provided guide and blog link to gain a clearer understanding of how instance strategy functions.

S/4 HANA Transformation journey:

S/4HANA transition journey : A practical guide to senior leadership

Instance strategy blog

Data architecture

Ensure you have a clear understanding of the data and analytics products and solutions provided by SAP, including offerings like SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Data Intelligence, SAP Analytics Cloud, etc.

SAP has recently released Data and Analytics advisory methodology and recommended going through the blog to get more details

Exam day

The examination lasts for a duration of 3 hours, encompassing a total of 40 questions, divided into two segments (22 Multiple Choice Questions and 18 questions based on a case study). Familiarize yourself with the case study in advance, which you can find here

  • Select the best answers for all questions, including those you may be unsure of, as you may not have the opportunity to review them later. However, for uncertain answers, flag those questions so that, if time allows (which it typically does in most cases), you can revisit and modify your answers if necessary. In my experience, out of the 40 questions, 25 were easy, 10 were of moderate difficulty, and 5 were very challenging. It is crucial to thoroughly prepare in all the skill areas mentioned above, as you need to answer at least 32 questions correctly out of 40 to obtain certification!

What’s next after the exam

  • With SAP's acquisition of LeanIX, the closed-loop scenario of Business and IT transformation becomes achievable through the integration of SAP Signavio with LeanIX. SAP Signavio captures all business capabilities, seamlessly integrating with LeanIX to formulate Data, Technology, and Application architectures. Therefore, it is highly recommended to enhance your skills in LeanIX.
  • Attaining certification from one of the hyperscalers (AWS, GCP, or Azure) is advisable, as many of our RISE with SAP customers utilize these platforms. Obtaining advanced architect certification in one of these hyperscalers enhances your ability to engage in customer discussions regarding the implementation of S/4HANA on hyperscaler landscapes.
  • Upskilling in cutting-edge technologies such as Generative AI, Metaverse, and the Internet of Things is beneficial, as many leaders are turning to enterprise architects for advice and guidance on transforming their businesses with these technologies and developing a roadmap for their adoption.

All the best and thanks for reading!!! Reach out to me if you have any questions

entry.title: Singapore, Madrid & Phoenix: SAP ICC attended the 3 SAP Partner Summit for SME locations!
entry.content:
The biggest, the best and the most remarkable event is back! SAP Partner Summit for SME, SAP's premier event for SME segment, took place in 3 beautiful cities:

  • Singapore: March 12 - 13, 2024
  • Phoenix (AZ): April 10 - 12, 2024
  • Madrid: April 23 - 25, 2024

These events not only serve as a platform for our partners to directly hear from SAP experts but also serve as a great networking opportunity where SAP partners often connect among each other for business opportunities and collaborative ideas. In 2024 the tonic of the events was taking SAP Business One into a Cloud future and keep addressing the lower mid-market and small businesses with SAP Cloud offers around  #SAPBTP and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (GROW with SAP).

SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) was present in the 3 events. In line with SAP's strategy it provides certifications for partner solutions which fit into the scenarios of GROW with SAP, SAP BTP and SAP Business One. As highlighted in an opening video recorded by Karl Fahrbach, SAP Chief Partner Officer, "when you build solutions with BTP, you can get them certified by SAP".

entry.title: Visualize Mobile Services Destination using SAP Mobile Backend Tools and OData CSDL Modeler
entry.content:
Visualize Mobile Services Destination using SAP Mobile Services Backend Tools and OData CSDL Modeler

SAP Mobile Services provides powerful platform services and SDKs in SAP BTP Platform for mobile developers to build mobile applications to consume SAP backend systems, which in many cases expose OData services. In SAP Mobile Services, these OData services are managed by creating mobile destinations under mobile applications. In this article, we will show you how to add a mobile services landscape to SAP Mobile Backend Tools as well as visualize the OData services from the mobile destinations using SAP Mobile Services OData CSDL Modeler.

Let's start with the "Purchase Order" service in Business Accelerator Hub. You can find more details from https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001/overview:

We can use the sandbox URL as a backend system URL in SAP Mobile Services. In the mobile services cockpit, create a mobile application called "purchaseorder":

Add a mobile destination in the newly created mobile application "purchaseorder" by using the "Mobile Connectivity" feature:

After the mobile destination has been created, you can ping the destination to ensure the backend connectivity is working:

Download mobile backend tools from https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPSE.vsc-extension-mbt and install it to visual studio code. Also in order to visualize the backend OData Services, you need to install SAP Mobile Services OData CSDL Modeler from https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPSE.vsc-extension-odata-csdl-modeler.

After both vscode extensions are installed, go to mobile services view in the sidebar panel:

In the mobile services sidebar panel, click "Add Landscape" button and open the new mobile services webview:

In the mobile services cockpit, go to "Important Links" view:

We will use the "Admin UI" URL when we add the mobile services landscape to mobile backend tools. Fill in all the required information for the fields in the webview:

Click "OK" button and add the mobile services landscape to mobile backend tools:

Click the "purchase" order mobile destination, you will be prompted with a vscode information box to enable Cross Context SSO for the mobile application. The mobile application needs to enable 'Cross Context SSO" so that we can use passcode to bypass the OAuth authentication:

In order to access the OData services through the mobile destination, we need to pass the OAuth authentication:

Open the browser and copy the passcode from the web page:

Paste the passcode to the information dialog and press enter key:

After mobile services passes the authentication, you should be able to see the backend odata services metadata document is loaded by the OData CSDL Modeler:

Go to the "Diagram" view, you should be able to visualize the backend odata services like below:

If you only want to see OData entity types, you can click the "Filter" toolbar button and uncheck the other types:

Conclusion

In this blog post, we have demonstrated how to use the combination of SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools and SAP Mobile Services OData CSDL Modeler for mobile developers and administrators to manage mobile services landscapes in mobile backend tools in Visual Studio Code environment, as well as how to visualize the mobile destination OData Services using OData CSDL Modeler.

entry.title: In production order output, how to generate multiple output items with different form templates.
entry.content:
In S/4HANA CLoud , in production order output, how to generate multiple output items with the same data source and different form templates.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: In production order output, how to generate multiple...
entry.content:
We can use the Receiver to generate multiple outputs items following below steps.

Take output type : ORDER_DATA_LIST as example.

  1. Create a new form template YY1_ORDER_DATA_LIST based on standard form template : PP_MOR_DATA_LIST in app Maintain Form Template.
  2. Assign the form template in SSCUI-102313 (Assign Form Templates)
  3. Go to app ‘Output Parameter Determination’ , choose Business Rule : ‘Manufacturing Order (Production Planning)’ and Determination Step : ‘Receiver’.
    Create different Roles for Output Type : ORDER_DATA_LIST then activate it.
  4. Go to Determination Step : ‘Form Template’, maintain different form templates per Roles then activate it.
    (Note: column ‘Role’ is hidden by default and needs to be enabled in the table settings.)
  5. Now, if creating production order, 2 output items will be generated automatically with different form templates.

entry.title: Execute OData Queries against Mobile Services using Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
In the previous blog post, we should already be able to access the OData services of the mobile destination using the combination of SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools and OData CSDL Modeler:

Now we will show you how to test the OData services by running OData queries using Mobile Backend Tools. 

First click "Run Test Query" toolbar button in the right top corner:

Now you should be able to see the test query page like below:

If you click the "Send" button, you will be able to see the service document of the backend OData services:

Now we can execute some OData query by specifying the OData query URLs, for example "PurchaseOrder?$filter=CreatedByUser eq 'CB9980000026' and PurchasingOrganization eq '1710' and Supplier eq '17300001' and PricingDocument eq '1000000340'"

Let's try some a bit more complex one like "PurchaseOrder?$filter=CreatedByUser eq 'CB9980000026' and PurchasingOrganization eq '1710' and Supplier eq '17300001' and PricingDocument eq '1000000340'&$expand=_PurchaseOrderItem,_PurchaseOrderNote":

And even more complex query like "PurchaseOrder?$filter=CreatedByUser eq 'CB9980000026' and PurchasingOrganization eq '1710' and Supplier eq '17300001' and PricingDocument eq '1000000340'&$expand=_PurchaseOrderItem($expand=_DeliveryAddress,_PurchaseOrderInvoicingPlan,_PurchaseOrderItemNote),_PurchaseOrderNote":

Like you can see, in the above scenarios the SAP Mobile Services Backend Tools will handle the complex authentication for you, and you just need to run your OData queries against the backend system through the mobile services destination inside the Visual Studio Code environment.

entry.title: Download OData Metadata Document from Mobile Services Destinations using Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
In the previous blog post, we have demonstrated how to open the OData services from a mobile destination using Mobile Backend Tools and OData CSDL Modeler.

If you want to download the OData metadata document of the backend system, you just need to click the "Download Metadata" toolbar button:

Save the metadata document to your local desktop and open it using Visual Studio Code:

Now you have the the backend OData metadata document locally in your PC.

entry.title: About Using SAP Companion for Ariba Guided Buying
entry.content:
In Ariba Guided Buying, we enabled SAP Companion to generate In-Application Help. However, when we started authoring the In-Application Help, we realized that each transaction in Ariba Guided Buying goes through various individual screens.

For example, creating a Purchase Requisition in Ariba Guided Buying goes through various individual screens. So, the In-App Guided Tour added on the first screen of the transaction is not displayed throughout all screens of the transaction. One reason could be - each screen in the transaction has unique context ID, so the Guided Tour created on the first screen does not appear on all screens. This may result in broken user experience for users. Is there a way to fix this?

Thank you in advance for the responses.

entry.title: Multiple Lines displayed in Bank Statement Items Details Report for the Same Field
entry.content:
In the report Bank Statement Items Details, the system shows 2 lines for the same entry, where in one line the entry is correct, and in the other it is shown as # (Not Assigned). For example, when the characteristics Open Item ID and the Transaction Amount are added, two lines are displayed: One with the Open Item ID and the respective Transaction Amount, and the other with the same Transaction Amount and the Open Item ID displayed as #. It is not clear why the second line is shown.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Lines displayed in Bank Statement Items Deta...
entry.content:
This behavior is expected for Combined data sources and all reports based on them.

In summary, when fields in the report view originate from different basic data sources, the report will display 2 lines: one showing the data from the first data source and the other showing the data from the second data source.

Here, the report Bank Statement Items Details (Report ID FINPAYU07_Q0001) is based on the Data Source FINPAYU07 which is a combined data source.

entry.title: Generate OData Client Code using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
In the previous blog post, we have demonstrated how to execute complex OData queries against a mobile destination of SAP Mobile Services using Mobile Backend Tools in a Visual Studio Code Environment. As a mobile developer, one of the major responsibilities of coding the mobile app is to code the OData access using programming languages like swift, kotlin and Java, etc. In this blog post, we will demonstrate how to generate OData client code directly from OData queries.

In SAP Mobile Backend Tools, open the mobile destination metadata document using OData CSDL Modeler, and then click the "Generate Test Client" toolbar button like below:

Choose a directory to hold the test client:

Click "Generate" button in the dialog, Mobile Backend Tools will generate the test client for this OData services:

After the generation has been complete, click "Open" button to open the test client in the specified directory:

You can open a test client called "PurchaseOrderFindByQuery" like below, you will be able to see the generate client code:

Inside Mobile Backend Tools environment, you can directly execute the test client. You can do that by clicking the "Execute" button:

And you will see the response from the backend system in the console after execution of the test client is complete:

You can also create a test client by supplying an OData query. In order to do that, click the "Add" button in the top right corner:

In the popup dialog, we can use "PurchaseOrder?$filter=CreatedByUser eq 'CB9980000026' and PurchasingOrganization eq '1710' and Supplier eq '17300001' and PricingDocument eq '1000000340'&$expand=_PurchaseOrderItem($expand=_DeliveryAddress,_PurchaseOrderInvoicingPlan,_PurchaseOrderItemNote),_PurchaseOrderNote" as the input:

Press enter key to accept the test case name, Mobile Backend Tools will generate the test client for this OData query in swift, kotlin and Java:

Click the "Execute" button to execute the test case, and you will be able to see the response from the backend system:

In summary, as a mobile developer, if you need to code a mobile app that needs to access the backend system through SAP Mobile Services using OData queries, you can generate the client code directly using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools as well as OData CSDL Tools and test the generated code directly in the Visual Studio Code environment.

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q1 2024 to Q2 2024
entry.content:
Image generated by DALL·E 3

We are once again back with our now regular “What’s New” blogs for SAP solutions for Three Lines (i.e. SAP Process Control, SAP Risk Management, SAP Audit Management and SAP Business Integrity Screening). As is now an old habit, Marie-Luise Wagener-Kirchner and Paul Petraschk from SAP GRC Product Management have shared their inputs and you will be able to discover below the new features delivered over these last few months.

This blog is only a short summary of selected enhancements delivered. But you will be able to find more details by following the links are mentioned at the end of this post.

 

Analytics for Audit Management

 

Getting to the relevant information quickly is what is important for most users. And auditors are not exempt from this finding – no pun intended!

With 13 analytical charts, the re-designed Audit Management Overview app together with the three new SAP Fiori apps: Analyze Audit Engagements, Analyze Findings, Analyze Action Plans, provide a powerful data visualization and analysis tool to help audit teams track their key metrics and gain insights into the internal auditing activities across the organization.

Furthermore, these screens are not only information display, they provide direct access to the underlying data with the click on a mouse.

Some examples of the analytical cards delivered include:

  • Audits by phase, by audit lead or audit manager, by attribute (status, type, group), by country or region
  • Audits with reports issues in the last 2 years
  • Overdue or upcoming audits
  • Open findings
  • Escalated or overdue action plans
  • … and more!

These visualizations help streamline the analysis, but also empower users with deeper insights into data for audit, finding, and action plan including visual representation and breakdowns.

Overview of the new embedded analytics in SAP Audit Management:

But wait, there’s more!

By default, the Audit Management Overview screen shows all the cards. That being said, users can personalize their overview page very easily and hide the cards they don't need.

From the “Manage Cards” option – the three dots at the top of all cards, they can simply use the toggles to hide/unhide cards. It’s that simple!

And, with the dimensions and filters, they can of course change the display, or the scope as they see fit.

In addition, SAP delivers out of the box “views” – in essence sets of predefined filtering criteria – but users can define their own filters and save them as custom views. These new views are then added to the list of standard delivered ones, and are applied as soon as they are selected. They can also be used as a default display if the user chooses.

More details and videos can be found on the dedicated page: - Audit Analytics | SAP Help Portal

 

Business Impact Analysis

 

Supporting Business Continuity Management requirements with planning, strategy definition, documentation, business impact analysis, as well as business continuity and disaster recovery plans has been a request from our SAP Risk Management customers for quite some time.

And you may recall that I advocated for the inclusion of Business Continuity Management within Three Lines for a long time (cf. for instance GRC Tuesdays: Is Business Continuity a Separate Activity?).

Well, I am very glad to announce that the first steps in this direction have been taken, and that SAP GRC Product Management and Engineering colleagues have released the first building block with the Business Impact Analysis capability in SAP Risk Management.

This module introduces a new option to classify an activity (or subprocess) as “Critical”. Subsequently, business continuity metrics can be maintained: Recovery Time Objective, Recovery Objective Point, and Criticality Level. From there, a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) survey can be run to collect inputs from expert stakeholders.

This new capability also comes with a Business Impact Analysis list report, and a dedicated response type.

This will help companies increase resilience by providing additional insights on operational risk and business continuity requirements.

Overview of the new Business Impact Analysis in SAP Risk Management:

 

More business continuity related features are planned as you will be able to see on the roadmap for SAP Risk Management, so keep an eye out for the next blogs as we detail the new capabilities:

 

More Information

 

As mentioned in introduction, these are only some of the enhancements delivered recently. For more details on what’s new, I would recommend having a look at the relevant sections of the dedicated Help Portals:

  • What's New in SAP Process Control
  • What’s New in SAP Risk Management
  • What’s New in SAP Audit Management
  • What’s New in SAP Business Integrity Screening

Previous “What’s New” blogs also provide some details on prior enhancements delivered. You will be able to find them at the following locations:

  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q3 2023 to Q1 2024
  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q2 2023 to Q3 2023
  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP Process Control and SAP Risk Management, Q1 2023 to Q2 2023

I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments either on this blog or on Twitter @TFrenehard and stay tuned for our next “What’s New” blog!

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Analyze Network Trace for SAP Asset and Service Manager using SAP Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
SAP Mobile Services provides an end to end network trace capability to record the network traffices and payload data to be analyze in order to find issues while using the mobile apps through SAP Mobile Services. Please refer to https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/tracing/admin/trace.html for more details about SAP Mobile Services network trace.

SAP Service and Asset Manager is a mobile asset management application that supports maintenance, service and safety technicians, as well as inventory management features. Please refer https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager for more details about SAP Service and Asset Manager.

In this blog post, we will show you how to analyze network trace for SAP Service and Asset Manager using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools in order to find out issues while using the app to access the SAP Backend Systems.

As IT consultants if your users who are using SAP Service and Asset Manager report issues to you, you can record the network trace activities using mobile services cockpit according to the instructions from mobile services network trace activities. In order to analyze the network trace data after the recording has been complete, you need to a network trace tool to analyzer the har file.

If you use Google chrome for example to open the network trace har file for SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile application, you will see a UI like below:

There are some limitations in Chrome used to analyze the network trace data for SAP Asset and Service mobile app:

  • There is no correlation view for the requests. SAP Mobile Services uses correlation ID to link the different stages of the same request.
  • SAP Asset and Service Manager uses lots of batch requests on mobile services side, and the user can't view the details of the each individual batch requests.
  • Also the user can't see the overall batch response payload at all in chrome.

In order to address the above issues, SAP Mobile Backend Tools offers a network trace analyzer to better visualize the network trace har file generated by SAP Mobile Services.

First download and install SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools in Visual Studio Code. After open the folder where the network trace har file is located, right click your network trace har file and select "Open With" context menu item in Visual Studio Code:

Select "MBT: Network Trace Analyzer":

The network trace for SAP Asset and Service Manager will be opened by Mobile Backend Tools:

By default, Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer only shows "Path", "Status", "Size" and "Time" columns in the UI, but you can configure more visible columns by clicking the filter like below:

Now we can do some analysis using Mobile Backend Tools. Open one of the batch requests like below:

Go to the "Payload" tab, you will see the request details:

and the response details:

And you can also view the response details in each individual batch request:

Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer also offers a correlation view so that all the records with the same correlation ID are grouped together to be treated as different stages for the same request:

and in this correlation view, it's very straightforward to see the different stages underneath of each request:

As a conclusion, in this blog post, we demonstrated how to analyze the network trace har file generated by SAP Mobile Services for SAP Asset and Service Manager mobile app using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer that includes specific optimization for har file in mobile services specific scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Calling APIs from S/4 to SF/EC using Cloud Connector
entry.content:
The SCC needs to be installed inside the same LAN (or even on the same host) as the HTTP server on which you want to call the APIs. It then opens a secure tunnel (like a VPN) from the server to the BTP. So the basic question is: can you install additional software like the SCC on the SF and EC systems?

If yes, calling the APIs via SCC is a matter of simple configuration, if no, I don't see a way how the SCC can be used here.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi @Molle,

thanks for your elaboration. I will definetly continue using the bgPF for this, let alone for the cloud-readiness and the unit-tests.

We were just searching for a way to execute illegal statements (e.g. SAP-functions that execute a SUBMIT or CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK) synchronously, being able to provide immediate feedback whether an operation was successful in a RAP-app.

I'm aware that this is exclusively an issue in on-prem/private cloud systems, though. If i had a wish-list however, i would be glad to see an easier way to pass the bgPF-process from modify-phase to save-phase as this seems to be necessary in every use within RAP.

entry.title: TMS - Use Transport Management Service (TMS) API
entry.content:
Series – TMS:

  • Transport Content Archives on Local File Systems with Transport Management Service (TMS)
  • SAP CI/CD Integration with Transport Management Service (TMS)
  • Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) and Content Agent Service (CAS)
  • Use Transport Management Service (TMS) API

Create a service instance of TMS with Standard plan, then create a service key:

 

Use service key details to obtain OAuth token to access TMS APIs:

Access Token URL: <url>/oauth/token
Client ID: <clientid>
Client Secret: <clientsecret>

Let's take some examples for your reference, for other APIs, please see: SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API.

 

Example - Get versions

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/versions

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "supportedVersions": [
        {
            "version": "1",
            "path": "v1"
        },
        {
            "version": "2",
            "path": "v2"
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get nodes

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "nodes": [
        {
            "id": 3,
            "name": "DEV_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": true,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [],
            "virtual": false
        },
        {
            "id": 6,
            "name": "PROD_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": false,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [
                {
                    "id": 7,
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "destinationName": "TMSTarget_CPICN40",
                    "importOptions": {
                        "strategy": "default"
                    }
                }
            ],
            "virtual": false
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get destinations

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/destinations

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Example - Get transport request

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

nodeId: Get from API <uri>/v2/nodes

status:

  • in - Initial
  • ru - Running
  • re - Repeatable
  • su - Succeeded
  • sk - Skipped
  • wa - Warning
  • er - Error
  • fa - Fatal
  • de - Deleted

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "transportRequests": [
        {
            "id": 305,
            "status": "initial",
            "archived": false,
            "position": 9,
            "createdBy": "c7cab313-bfcb-4eac-9386-08a841ee9d57",
            "createdByNamedUser": "377275f0-acbc-445e-8bff-1514da41db9f",
            "createdAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:14.707Z",
            "queuedAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:15.099Z",
            "description": "TiaTesttoTransportPackage - sample",
            "origin": "DEV_NODE",
            "entries": [
                {
                    "id": 308,
                    "storageType": "FILE",
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "uri": "299"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

Example - Import

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests/import

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Body:

{
  "namedUser": "Tia",
  "transportRequests": [
    305
  ]
}

Get transport request ID from the APIs like <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

Response: 

{
    "actionId": 280,
    "monitoringURL": "/v2/actions/280"
}

Example - Upload file from local to TMS

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/files/upload

Header:

  • 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'
  • 'file': file.zip
  • 'namedUser': 'Tia'

Useful Links

Help Portal - Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management Using APIs

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management Version Endpoint API

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio - License - Access
entry.content:
Hi Geschu,

It's great you chose SAP Signavio Process Insights to get wide and quick insights into your process performance, to better understand where and how to improve.
SAP Signavio Process Insights is a SaaS solution, running on SAP Business Technology Platform.


We have created a detailed onboarding guide so that you can connect your backend system with SAP Signavio Process Insights fully on your own.
During this onboarding process, you need to create a SAP BTP subaccount (in your global account) to provision SAP Signavio Process Insights there.
You are free to do everything manual or use the "booster" that automates and speeds up admin steps for you.
How to use the booster is also part of the documentation on help.sap.com, see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/BPI/f5642e5272c1465986661c763b56213d/96d61f7482bc478990545e7237a288a1.html

Best,
Robert

entry.title: Get Ready for a Data and AI Revolution: What's Hot at SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 - My personal picks!
entry.content:
Why SAP is Focusing on AI

SAP's strategic pivot towards AI is part of a comprehensive plan aimed at positioning the company for future growth. This initiative includes a significant investment in AI technologies to streamline operations and enhance efficiency. The creation of a dedicated AI growth area under the leadership of Dr. Philipp Herzig, SAP’s new Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer, underscores the centrality of AI in SAP’s future strategy. This move is designed to accelerate the development and deployment of AI solutions that can drive significant business value for SAP’s customers​ (SAP News Center)​​. You can find Dr. Herzig's session on SAPPHIRE here.

The Importance of SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere plays a crucial role in this AI-driven landscape. It represents a comprehensive data management solution that enables businesses to manage, integrate, and utilize their data more effectively. SAP Datasphere simplifies complex data environments by creating a unified data fabric, allowing organizations to access and leverage their data seamlessly. This capability is essential for maximizing the value of AI applications, which rely on high-quality, integrated data to function optimally​.

Datasphere's recent innovations, including the introduction of a knowledge graph and enhanced integration features, make it a pivotal tool for businesses looking to harness the power of data and AI. These advancements help companies transform their data into actionable insights, driving better decision-making and operational efficiency.

With close to 130 sessions at SAPPHIRE, the content can be a bit overwhelming. Below I've listed my top 10 sessions you should attend in my view.

Key Sessions on Data and AI

  1. Data in the Age of AI

    • Speakers: Irfan Khan, Dan Yu, Peter Howland
    • Overview: Discover how to maximize data value across SAP and third-party systems, with insights from Hershey's data transformation journey.
    • Session ID: BTP189

  2. Bringing AI from Prototype to Production

    • Speaker: Walter Sun
    • Overview: Learn about the journey of AI from prototype to production, focusing on identifying valuable AI applications and overcoming implementation challenges.
    • Session ID: TRE307

  3. Business Data Fabric: The Future of Data and AI

    • Speaker: Kaiser Larsen
    • Overview: Explore how SAP Datasphere simplifies data landscapes and empowers organizations.
    • Session ID: BTP151v

  4. SAP Datasphere: Looking Forward

    • Speaker: Torsten Ammon
    • Overview: Gain insights into new features and innovations in SAP Datasphere.
    • Session ID: BTP113

  5. Navigating the AI Era with SAP: Insights from the Executive Suite

    • Speakers: Andreas Welsch, Julia White, Michael Nilles, Walter Sun
    • Overview: Exclusive perspectives on the future of AI with SAP Business AI solutions.
    • Session ID: BAI220v

  6. Experience SAP Business AI in Action

    • Speaker: Richard Grandpierre
    • Overview: Demonstration of the Joule copilot and its impact on efficiency and decision-making.
    • Session ID: BAI900v

  7. Managing Your AI Risk: Responsible AI

    • Speaker: Dr. Bettina Laugwitz
    • Overview: Learn about mitigating AI bias and ensuring trust, privacy, and security.
    • Session ID: TRE312

  8. Learn About SAP's Responsible AI Practices

    • Speaker: Dr. Bettina Laugwitz
    • Overview: Understand SAP's commitment to ethical AI innovations.
    • Session ID: BAI206v

  9. Discover Recent and Upcoming AI Innovations for Business Functions

    • Speaker: Kai Muehlbauer
    • Overview: Learn about the Joule copilot AI Foundation and upcoming AI capabilities.
    • Session ID: BAI202

  10. Unlock the Potential of Your Data Assets: Data-Driven Decision-Making

    • Speaker: Frank Gundlich
    • Overview: Leverage technology and innovation to reshape decision-making processes.
    • Session ID: PAR309v

Conclusion

SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 is a great event for those interested in the latest advancements in Data and AI and SAP is going all in. With a diverse array of sessions, attendees will gain valuable insights into how these technologies can transform their businesses. Don't miss the opportunity to explore these sessions and stay ahead in the rapidly evolving landscape of Data and AI.

It's free, go for it.

For more information and to register for the event, visit the SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 event page and the virtual event page

entry.title: How to work with Logprobs that come from the Open AI models in the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Logprobs

If you are familiar with logits and probability distributions, but curious about log probs, this notebook is for you.

The source notebook can be found here: https://github.com/yaoshiang/logprobs/blob/main/Logprobs.ipynb

Why do we need to understand log-probs?

Many LLM APIs now expose log-probs of the tokens it generates. This is a high resolution estimate of confidence of the model.

Most importantly, you can use these probabilities to estimate the probability or confidence of a model when trying to judge its calibration.

For example, this is the official Open AI cookbook for working with log probs, and the log-probs allow you to use an LLM as a calibrated classifier:

https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/using_logprobs

The basics: sigmoid and softmax.

Before we get into log-probs, let's cover some basics.

Why do we use sigmoid and softmax as activation functions for classifiers?

Becuase they turn the output of a neural network model, which are in the range (-inf, inf), into the range (0, 1). And we can interpret numbers in the range (0, 1) as percentages or probabilities.

The outputs of a neural network before applying sigmoid or softmax are called logits.

Sigmoid

Let's make sure we can believe the statement that sigmoid turns logits, or numbers in the range (-inf, inf), into numbers in the range (0, 1). Let's pick some corner case numbers, run sigmoid, and see if the output is indeed in the range (0,1).

Look good! The logits do indeed get squished into the range (0, 1). This means that for the numbers that we get out of a neural network, we can run the sigmoid function and interpret it as a 1% chance, 27% chance, 50% chance, 73% chance, and 99% chance of being a positive.

Softmax

Softmax takes a set of logits and like sigmoid, squishes them into the range (0,1). It has another important attribute: the sum of the numbers will be 1.0, aka 100%. Let's test that empirically.

Suppose the output of a model classifying dogs, cats, and birds is 2.0, 1.0, and -1.0. Those clearly don't sum to one. And one of the values is negative! This is definitely not a probability distribution.

But after running softmax, we do get a probably distribution: each value is between zero and one, and the sum of 71%, 26%, and 4% is indeed 100%.

Softmax is invariant to scalar shifts.

Now let's see what happens if we shift these logits by a constant amount. Let's add 10 to all the logits.

 

Interesting! The output is the same! The fancy way of saying this is that the softmax function is invariant to constant shifts in the input logits.

Numerical Instability

However, just because softmax is mathematically invariant to constant shifts, doesn't mean we can go crazy when actually doing math on silicon. A 32 bit floating point number has about 7 significant digits (ask ChatGPT about mantissa and exponent if you wanna learn more).

So if we add big number, like 10,000,000, the computer will round away the 2, 1 and -1 and just see 10,000,000. And running softmax of a vector of three numbers, all of which are the same, will result in a uniform distribution: 33.3%, 33.3%, 33.3%.

The problem actually gets worse than that. When training a neural network, we run an algorithm called back propagation, which requires that we calculate gradients of a function. Gradients are the first-order derivative - the slope of a line. If the line is really flat, your gradients will round down to zero and your model won't train. If the line is really vertical, your gradients will approach infinity.

Unfortunately, the softmax function uses exponents, and the loss function of a model uses logs. Both of these functions can get very vertical and very flat at the extremes. This means that for the shifted logits, even if they should give the same numbers after running softmax, due to numerical instability, you'll instead get a NaN or inf.

Let's run a simple backprop algorithm in pytorch to prove this.

Notice that the gradients from the big logits (102 and 101) are NaN, or Not A Number. This means the values overflowed, which would ruin any neural network training.

Notice that the gradients from the big logits (102 and 101) are NaN, or Not A Number. This means the values overflowed, which would ruin any neural network training.

Diving into the math to find the numerically stable log-softmax function.

We are going to have to dig into the greek letters to figure out how to stabilize this math.

Softmax is defined as:

Where 𝑧_𝑖 is the logit for class 𝑖.

Notice all those exponents... running 𝑒^101 is what caused our previous code to overflow into NaNs.

Crossentropy is the loss function for a probability distribution, based on the basic theory of Maximum Likelihood Estimation which underpins neural network training. Crossentropy is defined as:

Where 𝑦𝑖 is really just a switch that turns on a specific probability 𝑝𝑖 for the true label (e.g. dog), and 𝑝𝑖 is the output of the softmax function.

Combining the two equations allows us to calculate crossentropy on a logit:

We know that the log(𝑎𝑏)=log(𝑎)−log(𝑏), and log(𝑒^𝑥)=𝑥 so we can adjust the above to

 Let's look deeper inside the key of that equation: it appears to be just a constant shift of all the logits, 𝑧 _1,𝑧_2,𝑧_3,...,𝑧_𝑘. In fact, that equation is the log-softmax of the logits.

We have freed the logits 𝑧_1,𝑧_2,𝑧_3,...,𝑧_𝑘 from that nasty log-exp combo, but, we haven't solved the calculation of the right hand side of the equation. We still see the log of the sum of the exponent of a series of numbers. Are we still stuck taking the exponent of big numbers?

Luckily, we have the log-sum-exp trick to stabilize that part of the equation.

Log-Sum-Exp

Anytime you are taking the log of the sum of the exponents, know that you can factor out some of the big numbers (e.g 𝑒^101) using the log-sum-exp technique.

First, we can immediately subtract a big number 𝑀 from all the logits, then multiply the resulting value by 𝑒𝑀.

 Then, we find the log of a product of two values, and can move that to be the sum of two logs.

And we know the log of an exponent of a value is just the value... 

so we have effectively reduced the scale of our logits while adding just a constant value to the log-sum-exp of the reduced logits. Voila! Numerical stability.

The log-sum-exp trick uses the maximum M, but we need a specific M that generates the exact log-probs - but since our numbers are numerically stable, we can calculcate the log-sum-exp exactly a second time without using this trick, to essentially get a second 𝑀 value to add to the logits to find the log-probs.

Nice Properties of Log-Probs

The log prob output by APIs like Open AI are really just the logits shifted by a constant amount so that they have some nicer properties than raw logits.

Since probabilities are always between zero and one, the log-prob will always be less than zero. A high confidence prediction would have a prob of say 0.999, yielding a log-prob of something like -1e-3. A low confidence prediction would have a log prob that looks something like -0.5.

If you want to calculate the actual probability of a specific token, just take the exponent of the log-prob. No need for worrying about the other [30,521] possible tokens that the model output to calculate a sum - a single log-prob for a single token is all you need to know the probability of that token.

Log probs always can be flowed into a loss function almost directly, without need for logs or exponents or crossentropy or softmax. The loss function for this in pytorch is

https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.nn.NLLLoss.html

Testing log probs

Let's try to prove some of the math above. Let's set up some logits, calculate the log-softmax. We see that indeed, all the values are negative.

Now let's see if the logits and the log-probs output the same probability distribution. Yup, that checks out too! 

Finally, let's prove that we can calculate the probabilities simply by running the exp function, not the softmax, when we have the log_probs. Once again, things look good. 

 Finally, let's make sure that this is indeed numerically stable. Can we get good gradients with big numbers? Recall that we previously ran a naive approach to calculcating this gradient and got NaN for gradients.

Yup! We got good gradients despite large logits! 

SAP Generative AI Hub

If you're an SAP customer, you may be accessing Open AI models through SAP Generative AI Hub.

According to the documentation there, when you access Open AI models there, you are actually accessing Azure Open AI models and indeed, the SAP documentation points you to the Azure Open AI docs.

And Azure Open AI, like the direct Open AI API, gives you the ability to access the log-probs outputted by an LLM like GPT-4o. With those logprobs, you can build calibrated classifiers and maybe even double check if your chatbot answers aren't as good as they could be.

Advanced loss functions in TensorFlow

If you've been programming in Tensorflow, you might be a little worried right now - you've been activating your neural networks with Softmax and then using the categorical_crossentropy loss function, just like you were taught in that basic ML course. Were you risking numerical instability?

TF/Keras ignores what you implemented and operates directly on the logits. See this code block:

https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/e7e2b655d573de99c7c7fd2ceb5659f57fb2d908/tensorflow/python/keras/backend.py#L4762

Then, when it's operating on the logits, it uses the log_softmax function to apply the constant shift of the logits by the numerically stable implementation of log-sum-exp.

https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/e7e2b655d573de99c7c7fd2ceb5659f57fb2d908/tensorflow/python/ops/nn_ops.py#L4149C8-L4149C49

entry.content:

Use service key details to obtain OAuth token to access TMS APIs:

Access Token URL: <url>/oauth/token
Client ID: <clientid>
Client Secret: <clientsecret>

Let's take some examples for your reference, for other APIs, please see: SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API.

 

Example - Get versions

Method: GET

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "supportedVersions": [
        {
            "version": "1",
            "path": "v1"
        },
        {
            "version": "2",
            "path": "v2"
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get nodes

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "nodes": [
        {
            "id": 3,
            "name": "DEV_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": true,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [],
            "virtual": false
        },
        {
            "id": 6,
            "name": "PROD_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": false,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [
                {
                    "id": 7,
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "destinationName": "TMSTarget_CPICN40",
                    "importOptions": {
                        "strategy": "default"
                    }
                }
            ],
            "virtual": false
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get destinations

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/destinations

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Example - Get transport request

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

nodeId: Get from API <uri>/v2/nodes

status:

  • in - Initial

  • ru - Running

  • re - Repeatable

  • su - Succeeded

  • sk - Skipped

  • wa - Warning

  • er - Error

  • fa - Fatal

  • de - Deleted

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "transportRequests": [
        {
            "id": 305,
            "status": "initial",
            "archived": false,
            "position": 9,
            "createdBy": "c7cab313-bfcb-4eac-9386-08a841ee9d57",
            "createdByNamedUser": "377275f0-acbc-445e-8bff-1514da41db9f",
            "createdAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:14.707Z",
            "queuedAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:15.099Z",
            "description": "TiaTesttoTransportPackage - sample",
            "origin": "DEV_NODE",
            "entries": [
                {
                    "id": 308,
                    "storageType": "FILE",
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "uri": "299"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

Example - Import

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests/import

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Body:

{
  "namedUser": "Tia",
  "transportRequests": [
    305
  ]
}

Get transport request ID from the APIs like <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

Response: 

{
    "actionId": 280,
    "monitoringURL": "/v2/actions/280"
}

Example - Upload file from local to TMS

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/files/upload

Header:

  • 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

  • 'file': file.zip

  • 'namedUser': 'Tia'

Useful Links

Help Portal - Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management Using APIs

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management Version Endpoint API

entry.title: Newly added approver unable to see old pending PRs for approval
entry.content:
A newly added approver in the approval group Category Manager - IT Software cannot see previous pending PRs for approval in their To-do list. 

I want to confirm the expected behavior below.

  1. If the approval flow of the PR is already Active for approval of Category Manager - IT Software, then the newly added users in this group will not be able to see it in their To-do list.
  2. Suppose the approval flow is pending approval of other approvers before the approval of the said group. In that case, the newly added user can see the PRs in their To-do list once the approval flow is Active for approval of the said group.
  3. Or may we know the standard behavior of the system for those newly added users in the group?

We also want to know how to trigger those PRs that are not in included in their To-do list, however, when they checked the PR manually they can able to see the Approve button into it.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Newly added approver unable to see old pending PRs fo...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

My name is Lisa from the MM-PUR team. Let me explain how the workflow approver determination works:

When the workflow(traditional or flexible workflow) is triggered, the system will determine the approvers. In your case they are determined based on the category IT Software. These approvers are set and assigned in the workflow log. There is no job that runs in the background to check if these have been changed/updated. They are determined at the time of workflow triggering initially and not again unless the actual document for example Purchase Order is updated and workflow is reset/retriggered. 

So, if a new approver is added in the workflow settings(category settings) and a document is already in Awaiting Approval status, the new approver will not see these documents in their Approval list. This is standard behaviour. The workflow has already been triggered and the original approver is determined. The only way to ensure the new approver now see's these documents is to forward the pending approval documents to the new approver. 

For any document that has not yet triggered the workflow, these will go to the new approver as the workflow has not yet ran and when it does the new approver will be determined as expected. 

I hope that helps answer your query. 

Kind regards,
Lisa
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Hybris 2211 : SmartEdit date picker prefilled with th...
entry.content: The ActiveTill field is an optional field, right? So I suppose a change to Date/Time field to NOT automatically add date/time as long as this date/time field is Optional could help here.
